message:
chore(ci,clippy): add GPUI system deps, fix wasm RNG, apply clippy suggestions

This commit resolves CI build failures and applies clippy lints for more
idiomatic Rust code across the codebase.

## CI Infrastructure

**.github/workflows/ci.yml** (+14 lines):
- Added GPUI system dependencies for Ubuntu runners:
  * libfontconfig1, libfreetype6-dev (font rendering)
  * libx11-xcb-dev, libxcb1-dev (X11 window system)
  * libxkbcommon-dev, libxkbcommon-x11-dev (keyboard input)
- Applied to both "Checks" and "Replay Determinism" jobs
- Fixes linking errors when building scriptbots-render with GPUI

**crates/scriptbots-web/Cargo.toml** (+3 lines):
- Added getrandom = { version = "0.2.16", features = ["js"] } for wasm32
- Enables JavaScript-based RNG when targeting WebAssembly
- Resolves wasm-pack build failures from missing entropy source

## Code Quality (Clippy Fixes)

**crates/scriptbots-core/src/lib.rs** (+25/-20):
- Removed unused imports: CardinalDirections, serde_json
- Replaced redundant closures: .map(compile_tile) instead of .map(|t| compile_tile(t))
- Changed .unwrap_or_else(|| deterministic_fn()) to .unwrap_or(deterministic_fn())
  for functions without side effects (clippy::unnecessary_lazy_evaluation)
- Refactored index-based loop to iterator: .iter().take(len) instead of for idx in 0..len
- Improved formatting for long function chains

**crates/scriptbots-app/src/terminal/mod.rs** (+12/-14):
- Replaced write!(file, "\n") with idiomatic writeln!(file)
- Refactored auto-pause condition checks using Option::filter():
  * Before: nested if let Some(limit) { if value >= limit { ... } }
  * After: .filter(|&limit| value >= limit)
- Reduces nesting and makes intent clearer

**crates/scriptbots-render/src/lib.rs** (+23/-27):
- Applied same auto-pause refactoring as terminal (using let-chains and filter())
- Flattened nested conditionals for better readability
- Reduced cognitive complexity in update_simulation() hot path

**crates/scriptbots-app/src/main.rs** (+3/-1):
- Changed if let Some(seed) = var.ok() to if let Ok(seed) = var (more idiomatic)
- Added det_check: None to test fixture structs for AppCli compatibility

**crates/scriptbots-app/src/bin/control_cli.rs** (+1/-4):
- Simplified .unwrap_or_else(|_| default.to_string()) to .unwrap_or(default.to_string())
- Removed unnecessary closure allocation

## Documentation

**PLAN_TO_PORT_SCRIPTBOTS_TO_MODERN_IDIOMATIC_RUST_USING_GPUI.md** (+1/-1):
- Updated modcounter cadence task status to "Currently In Progress"
- Reflects active work on tick scheduler implementation

## Summary

These changes improve CI reliability by ensuring required system libraries are
installed before building GPUI components, fix wasm builds by providing proper
RNG entropy, and apply standard Rust idioms throughout the codebase per clippy
recommendations. No functional behavior changes—all modifications are refactorings
for code quality and build infrastructure.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -36,11 +36,18 @@ jobs:
       - name: Checkout
         uses: actions/checkout@v4
 
-      - name: Install mold linker
+      - name: Install mold linker and system deps
         if: ${{ matrix.os == 'ubuntu-24.04' }}
         run: |
           sudo apt-get update
-          sudo apt-get install -y mold
+          sudo apt-get install -y \
+            mold \
+            libfontconfig1 \
+            libfreetype6-dev \
+            libx11-xcb-dev \
+            libxcb1-dev \
+            libxkbcommon-dev \
+            libxkbcommon-x11-dev
 
       - name: Install Rust toolchain
         uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master

@@ -198,7 +198,7 @@
   - [Completed - GPT-5 Codex 2025-10-22: differential drive locomotion aligned with legacy wheel outputs and boost scaling] Align locomotion with differential drive physics: interpret outputs[0]/[1] as wheel velocities, derive pose updates/boost scaling from the original formulas, and validate wrapping math against `processOutputs`.
   - [Completed - GPT-5 Codex 2025-10-23: herbivore attack gating in combat, diet-weighted carcass/food handling, and reproduction rates split via `reproduction_rate_herbivore/carnivore`; tests cover herbivore gains and carnivore waste] Port herbivore vs carnivore behaviors: enforce attack restrictions for herbivores, replicate reproduction timers (`REPRATEH/REPRATEC`), and apply diet-based modifiers in food intake and carcass sharing.
   - [Completed - GPT-5 Codex 2025-10-23: ground food intake/waste modeled with speed scaling and herbivore tendency; integration tests added for herbivore gain and carnivore waste] Match food consumption math: reuse `FOODINTAKE`, `FOODWASTE`, and speed-dependent gains tied to wheel speeds and herbivore tendency, updating tests to cover stationary vs. fast agents.
-  - [ ] Restore modcounter cadence: introduce configurable tick scheduler for aging every 100 ticks, periodic charts/reporting, reproduction gating randomness, and guard against regressions with deterministic seeds.
+  - [Currently In Progress - 2025-10-23 Codex] Restore modcounter cadence: introduce configurable tick scheduler for aging every 100 ticks, periodic charts/reporting, reproduction gating randomness, and guard against regressions with deterministic seeds.
   - [Completed - GPT-5 Codex 2025-10-23: per-agent `mutation_rates` (primary/secondary), `temperature_preference`, and trait modifiers are inherited/mixed and mutated during reproduction with lineage logging] Carry mutation parameter genetics: track per-agent `mutrate1/2`, `temperature_preference`, and trait modifiers, mutating them during reproduction following the C++ meta-mutation rules.
   - [ ] Build regression tests comparing Rust tick traces against captured C++ snapshots.
 - **Brain Implementations**
